title: Hydraulic Disc Brake — Four-Wheel Emergency Stop
design_question: How to provide high-force friction braking at all wheel speeds including standstill in a four-wheeler?
selected_approach: Four-pot caliper hydraulic disc brake on each wheel. Master cylinder (bore 19 mm) driven by brake pedal via 6:1 lever ratio. ABS modulator (Bosch 8.0) cycles solenoid valves at 12 Hz when wheel lock detected by Hall-effect wheel-speed sensors. Emergency brake controller (Arduino Mega) monitors accelerometer for crash detection and triggers solenoid boost valve.
rationale: Hydraulic disc brakes deliver maximum braking force at zero speed (unlike eddy-current brakes). ABS integration prevents lock-up. DIY-compatible: donor vehicle calipers, master cylinder, and hydraulic lines. Stopping distance < 20 m from 60 km/h.

alternatives.reason_rejected: Prone to fade, heavier, less heat dissipation.

verification_notes: FACT: 'Hydraulic Disc Brake — Four-Wheeler Emergency Stop' gives stopping distance d = v²/(2µg). FACT: 'Anti-lock Braking System (ABS)' confirms slip ratio 0.1–0.2.
supporting_fact_titles: Hydraulic Disc Brake — Four-Wheeler Emergency Stop
Anti-lock Braking System (ABS) — Wheel Slip Control
